Mission

⏱ 2 min • Governance

Why this exists and how content stays honest.

📌 Summary
Why this exists and how content stays honest.

Why this exists
One place for AEM job search in India — companies, apply steps, and interview prep.

  • Only add what can be backed up with a link
  • Keep it useful long-term, not just for one job hunt
  • Write clearly — no filler
  • Fix and improve as more is learned

Career Strategy

⏱ 4 min • Career

Where AEM jobs show up in India and how to pick companies.

📌 Summary
Where AEM jobs show up in India and how to pick companies.

In India, AEM roles show up at product companies, GCCs, and agencies / SIs — each is different in pace, ownership, and interviews. Use Companies to shortlist, then Resume & LinkedIn templates to prep your profile, then How to Apply for day-to-day steps.

Build strong knowledge in the AEM areas you actually work with, such as Sites, Cloud Service, Dispatcher, Forms, or integrations. You do not need to present yourself as an expert in every frontend, backend, cloud, and Adobe technology.

Even when you're not switching jobs, stay ready: keep LinkedIn/Naukri current, periodically review interview questions or take an interview when you are genuinely open to exploring a role, and fix whatever gaps show up. This isn't a ranking — every company has trade-offs.

AEM engineer (individual contributor)

From building components to owning Dispatcher, Cloud Manager, and performance.

  1. Foundation — HTL, Core Components, basic Sling Models, author/publish setup.
  2. Platform depth — Dispatcher, caching, OSGi services, Cloud Manager pipelines.
  3. Senior scope — Headless fragments, GraphQL, performance tuning, helping other teams on technical calls.

Solution / platform architect

Plan big AEM rollouts across brands and connect AEM to Analytics, Target, and AEP.

  1. Integration patterns — AEM with Analytics, Target, AEP; identity and consent setup.
  2. Scale and MSM — MSM blueprints, content workflows, CI/CD and environment planning.
  3. Modern stack — Edge Delivery, Universal Editor, headless and personalization at the edge.

Resume & LinkedIn templates

⏱ 3 min • Branding

Copy-paste patterns for resume, LinkedIn, Naukri, and outreach.

📌 Summary
Copy-paste patterns for resume, LinkedIn, Naukri, and outreach.

Present yourself as an AEM Developer with clear skills in Sites, Cloud Service, Dispatcher, and integrations. Fill in your real projects and numbers — don't make up impact. Use these as writing patterns, not ready-made experience claims. Replace every placeholder with work you actually performed and can explain properly in an interview. Templates below are for LinkedIn, Naukri, outreach messages, and resume bullets.

Resume bullet — AEM delivery impact

resume

Delivered [feature/migration you actually worked on] on AEM as a Cloud Service for [brand/program], improving [metric you can explain, e.g. publish lead time / cache hit ratio / Core Web Vitals] by [your measured change] through [Dispatcher tuning / pipeline automation / component refactor you did].

Resume bullet — headless / GraphQL

resume

Implemented headless content APIs using AEM Content Fragments and persisted GraphQL queries, enabling [web/mobile] teams to [specific outcome you delivered] without duplicate content entry.

STAR story frame — production incident

interview

Situation: [outage/symptom]. Task: [your role]. Action: [triage steps, collaboration, fix]. Result: [restored SLA, post-mortem, preventive change].

LinkedIn summary pattern — AEM developer

branding

AEM Developer with hands-on experience in [Sites/Assets/Cloud Service] and [Dispatcher/headless/personalization]. [One sentence on a real project outcome you can explain — e.g. reliable releases, faster publish cycle, or integration you owned.] Background in [Java/frontend/platform] and [industry]. Open to AEM roles in [city/remote].

Recruiter outreach — AEM roles

networking

Hi [Name], this is [Your name]. I am an AEM [level] developer with experience in [Cloud Service / Dispatcher / headless]. I am exploring [company/sector] roles where Adobe Experience Manager is central to the stack. Open to a brief conversation if this aligns with roles you are hiring for in [team/location].

Naukri profile headline & summary — AEM

branding

Headline: AEM Developer | [Sites / AEMaaCS / Dispatcher] | [City / Remote] Summary: AEM developer with [N] years on Adobe Experience Manager ([Sites/Assets/Cloud Service]). [1–2 outcomes from your real work that you can explain in an interview.] Comfortable with Java, Sling/HTL, and [Dispatcher / headless / integrations you have used]. Open to AEM roles in [locations]. Prefer roles where AEM is core to the product, not a side CMS.

Resume bullet — Dispatcher / caching

resume

Improved publish-side performance for [site/program you worked on] by tuning Dispatcher [filter/cache/invalidation] rules and measuring [cache hit ratio / TTFB / error rate you tracked], reducing [symptom you observed] without changing authoring workflows.

Core Skills

⏱ 5 min • Skills

AEM technologies worth knowing, with doc links.

📌 Summary
AEM technologies worth knowing, with doc links.

Start with the basics (Sites, Sling, HTL, OSGi, Dispatcher, Java for AEM), then move to Cloud Service, headless, and Experience Platform hooks. The table links to official Adobe and Apache docs — use those instead of random blog posts when you're studying.

TechnologyCategoryLevelSummary
AEM Core ComponentsComponentsBeginnerAdobe-maintained component library with accessibility and Cloud Service compatibility.
  • AEM Core Components
HTL (Sightly)TemplatingBeginnerHTML Template Language for secure, readable AEM component markup.
  • HTL (Sightly) scripting — Apache Sling
AEM AssetsDAMIntermediateDigital asset management, metadata schemas, renditions, and brand portals.
  • AEM Assets videos and tutorials
AEM DispatcherCachingIntermediateApache-based caching and security filter in front of AEM publish tiers.
  • Dispatcher configuration reference
AEM SitesCMSIntermediateEnterprise web content management: pages, templates, components, and multi-site authoring.
  • Adobe Experience Manager product documentation
  • WKND Developer Tutorial
Page 1 of 3

Learning Roadmap

⏱ 3 min • Learning

Step-by-step AEM topics with doc links.

📌 Summary
Step-by-step AEM topics with doc links.

New or rusty? Start with AEM basics (architecture → Sling/HTL → Dispatcher). Next: Cloud Service and Edge. When you need personalization / data: Experience Platform. Each step has Adobe / Apache doc links — go at your own speed.

How to Apply

⏱ 5 min • Apply

Resume, LinkedIn, Naukri, referrals, and notice period — step by step.

📌 Summary
Resume, LinkedIn, Naukri, referrals, and notice period — step by step.

Practical steps for staying job-ready on Indian portals — not only during active search. Covers notice period, outreach, and learning from every interview. Resume templates are in Resume & LinkedIn templates above.

Apply workflow

Owner

Start with your resume and profiles before you begin applying. When you find a role, check the expected joining date first. If the company needs someone immediately and you still have two or three months of notice period left, focus on other openings unless the recruiter says they can wait.

  1. Draft your resume with an AI assistant if useful: Share your actual experience — companies, projects, technologies, education, and employment history. Clearly mention the AEM role you are targeting, such as backend, full-stack, Forms, or platform-focused roles. Also mention the type of companies you want to target. If you use an AI assistant, ask it to prepare a role-focused draft and include relevant ATS keywords only where they match your real experience. Review every bullet yourself. Remove anything you did not actually do or cannot explain properly in an interview.
  2. Do not publish an AI-generated resume draft as-is. If you use Overleaf, start with a simple one-column template and move the reviewed content into it carefully. Check formatting, dates, links, section order, and every bullet before exporting the PDF. Do not add GitHub, HackerRank, or other profile links unless you actively use them.
  3. Upload the final PDF to an AI assistant and check it with one or two ATS checker tools. Do not focus only on the score because different tools can give different results. Use the report mainly to check whether your skills are being read correctly, important AEM keywords are missing, or any section is not parsed properly.
  4. Once the resume is ready, update LinkedIn and Naukri section by section. Your job title, company dates, skills, notice period, and experience should match the information in your resume. Keep your last working day, current location, preferred locations, CTC, and availability accurate. Use clear AEM skill names that match the work you actually do and the roles you are applying for. Do not add a skill only because it appears in a job description.
  5. Notice period — focus on roles that match your joining date: If you are serving a long notice period and a job clearly asks for an immediate joiner, check whether the recruiter or company can wait. If they cannot, it is usually better to skip that opening and spend your time on roles that match your joining date.
  6. A long notice period does not mean you should stop applying. Some companies have longer interview and approval processes, while others need people quickly. Read the job post, ask the recruiter about the expected joining date, and clearly mention your last working day.
  7. As your last working day gets closer, increase your daily job search. More immediate-joiner and early-joiner roles may now match your availability. Use early notice time to prepare your resume, LinkedIn, Naukri, company shortlist, and AEM interview prep.

Before applying, check the expected joining date. If the company needs an immediate joiner and you still have a long notice period, focus on other roles unless they mention notice-period flexibility.

Outreach

Owner

Do not depend only on job portals. If you find the recruiter who posted the role, an HR email in the job post, or someone who can refer you, contact them as well.

  1. LinkedIn posts: Search AEM (or your role keyword), filter type to Posts. First pass — wider time window; after that — last 1 day only. Look for HR or hiring posts for your role. If the recruiter has shared an email address in the post, send your resume there as well. Mention the role, your AEM experience, current location, and joining availability clearly. Keep the message short.
  2. Gmail cover-letter template: For LinkedIn or Naukri invites, keep a reusable Gmail template — short cover letter with skills, experience, LWD if applicable, contact details, and resume attached. Reuse for every invite-style apply. State your last working day clearly when the role has specific joining expectations.
  3. Referral before direct apply: When you see a job on LinkedIn or Naukri, do not apply immediately. Search that company on LinkedIn, find people in the same role (or a related AEM role), and send a connection request. If you know someone in the company, or find an AEM developer from that company on LinkedIn, you can politely ask whether they are comfortable referring you. Share the job link and a short summary of your experience. If they do not reply, continue with the official application instead of waiting.
  4. Recruiter direct: If the post lists a recruiter email or contact info, send your resume there as well alongside any portal application.
  5. Staffing / HR agencies: If a staffing agency is officially handling the role, follow the process shared by the recruiter. Before sending personal documents, check the recruiter, agency, company, and job details. Also check whether the same role is available on the company's careers site.

Do not depend only on job portals. If you find the recruiter who posted the role, an HR email in the job post, or someone who can refer you, contact them as well. Still use the company's official application process where required. Keep templates in Gmail; refine referral and HR-post wording with an AI assistant once, then reuse.

Train your LinkedIn feed

Owner

Keep your LinkedIn activity focused on AEM so it becomes easier to find relevant recruiters, hiring posts, groups, and job discussions.

  1. Join AEM-focused LinkedIn groups (see Community links on this page) and turn on notifications for the most active ones.
  2. Follow AEM recruiters, Adobe Experience Cloud professionals, AEM developers, architects, and companies you want to work for. When you find useful AEM job posts, save the search terms and check them regularly.
  3. Do not depend only on the LinkedIn home feed. Search for terms such as AEM Developer, Adobe Experience Manager, AEM Cloud Service, AEM Forms, and Adobe Experience Cloud. Try location-specific searches as well.
  4. Search AEM (Posts + Jobs) daily with a last-1-day filter once your notice window is open. Save useful searches and repeat them regularly instead of relying only on the home feed.

The goal is simple: when you open LinkedIn for job search, you should know where to look instead of scrolling through unrelated posts.

Careers sites vs job boards

Owner

AEM openings can appear across employer careers sites, LinkedIn, Naukri, recruiter posts, and other job boards. The same opening may not be equally visible on every channel.

  1. Do not check only the company careers page. AEM openings can also appear on LinkedIn, Naukri, recruiter posts, and other job boards. The same role may not be easy to find on every platform.
  2. In the Companies section, open the company details and check the AEM evidence and Hiring evidence links. These links explain why the company is included in the database and may also help you find recent AEM-related openings.
  3. Keep Naukri and LinkedIn profiles complete and current so inbound outreach can find you when the careers site is quiet.
  4. Check the official careers page for the role details, but also search LinkedIn, Naukri, and relevant recruiter posts.

Check the official careers page for the role details, but also search LinkedIn, Naukri, and relevant recruiter posts. Use the evidence links in this playbook to understand why a company is listed and where AEM-related hiring was found.

Learning sources

Owner

Keep job-search prep and AEM technical prep separate.

  1. Job-search content: An AI assistant can help draft resume bullets, profile copy, and outreach messages. Provide only your real experience and remove any claim you cannot verify or explain.
  2. AEM technical depth: This playbook's roadmaps, glossary, and interview prep — plus Adobe Experience League when you need to verify a topic before an interview.
  3. Employer research: Use the Companies table and company evidence before you interview or message someone at that employer.
  4. Do not list tools or platforms on your resume or profiles unless you use them actively.

AI tools can help structure and review your profile. This playbook focuses on AEM career research, learning paths, and interview preparation.

Daily and weekly job search routine

Owner

How to search and apply on LinkedIn and Naukri after your profiles are ready.

  1. Check new roles regularly: Check your saved AEM searches on LinkedIn, Naukri, and the company pages you are actively tracking. You do not need to apply to every opening. First check the skills, location, experience range, and expected joining date.
  2. Keep your profiles updated: Review LinkedIn and Naukri whenever your notice period, last working day, location, skills, or resume changes. The information across your resume and profiles should remain consistent.
  3. Follow up without waiting forever: If you contacted a recruiter or asked for a referral, keep track of it. Follow up once when appropriate, but continue applying to other roles instead of waiting for one company.
  4. Prepare while applying: Keep revising AEM topics and interview questions while you apply. If your resume mentions Dispatcher, AEM Forms, Solr, Cloud Service, or another specialised area, prepare those topics properly because interviewers may ask about your real project work.
  5. Increase applications as your joining date gets closer: Start preparing early. As your last working day gets closer, spend more time applying to new roles and contacting recruiters for openings that match your joining date.

Start preparing early. As your last working day gets closer, spend more time applying to new roles and contacting recruiters for openings that match your joining date.

Tools

Owner

Common tools for resume, profiles, and applications in India.

  1. ChatGPT or another AI assistant can help you draft and review resume content, LinkedIn or Naukri sections, and recruiter messages. Always check the final text against your real experience before publishing or sending it.
  2. Overleaf — one-column LaTeX resume template; manual paste from the draft, export PDF.
  3. ATS checker tools can help you find parsing problems, missing keywords, or badly structured sections. Different tools may give different scores, so do not treat one score as a hiring result.
  4. LinkedIn — profile, job search (30-day then 1-day filter), Posts search for HR hiring, company people search for referrals.
  5. Keep your Naukri profile consistent with your resume and LinkedIn. Update your notice period, last working day, preferred locations, skills, and availability when these details change.
  6. Gmail — saved template for cover letter and resume when applying via invite or email from a post.
  7. This playbook — Companies, filters, and interview prep once conversations start.

Avoid automated mass-application workflows. Use a reviewed base resume and consistent profile information, then adjust role-specific keywords or emphasis only when the changes accurately reflect your experience.

Community

Owner

Independent LinkedIn groups for AEM developers — discovery only; not run by this playbook.

  1. Open the Community section (sidebar → Community) for LinkedIn group links. These are independent peer groups — not administered here and not affiliated with Adobe.
  2. Join only if the group's rules fit you. Share openings and ask questions politely — do not spam resumes into discussions.
  3. Combine group leads with the Companies list and your own LinkedIn/Naukri search.

Community links live in the Community chapter so they stay in one place (also linked from How to Apply).

Open the Community section for independent LinkedIn group links (discovery only).

Glossary

⏱ 6 min • Reference

Short definitions for AEM and DXP terms used here.

📌 Summary
Short definitions for AEM and DXP terms used here.

Quick definitions for terms you'll see in interviews and roadmaps. For full detail, use Adobe Experience League and Apache docs.

TermDefinitionRelated
Adobe Experience Manager (AEM)Enterprise content management system for websites, assets, forms, and headless content. The core Adobe product AEM developers work with on Adobe Experience Cloud.dxp, aem-cloud
Digital Experience Platform (DXP)Integrated suite of products (CMS, DAM, analytics, personalization) used to design and deliver customer experiences across channels.aem, aep
AEM as a Cloud Service (AEMaaCS)Cloud-hosted AEM with continuous delivery, auto-scaling, and Adobe-managed infrastructure. Replaces self-hosted 6.5 for new programs.cloud-manager, dispatcher
Cloud ManagerAdobe pipeline tool for building, deploying, and monitoring AEM Cloud Service environments (dev, stage, prod).aem-cloud
Apache SlingRESTful OSGi framework AEM uses to map URLs to resources and scripts (JCR nodes).osgi, jcr

Interview Prep

⏱ 10 min • Interview

Technical and behavioural questions to practice.

📌 Summary
Technical and behavioural questions to practice.

AEM interview questions usually depend on your experience and the skills mentioned in your resume. If you have Dispatcher, AEM Forms, Solr, or Cloud Service in your resume, be ready for questions from those areas.

Use the questions below to check your preparation. Do not memorise answers word by word. Try to explain each topic using work you have actually done in a project. If you have not worked on a topic directly, understand the basics and be clear about your level of experience.

AI tools can help you revise a topic or compare your answer with a better explanation, but do not memorise wording that you cannot explain when the interviewer asks a follow-up question.

CategoryLevelQuestionGuidance
technicalBeginnerHow does HTL help prevent XSS compared to JSP scriptlets?Explain automatic context-aware escaping and separation of logic into Sling Models.
technicalBeginnerWhat is the JCR in AEM, and how does Oak relate to it?JCR is the hierarchical content API; Oak is the Jackrabbit Oak implementation AEM uses. Talk nodes/properties, workspaces at a high level, and that queries need appropriate indexes — avoid inventing storage internals you have not used.
technicalBeginnerWhy adopt Core Components instead of only custom components?Maintenance, accessibility, upgrade path, Cloud Service compatibility, extension via overlay.
behavioralBeginnerWhy AEM development instead of generic full-stack?Tie to depth in AEM and CMS platforms, business impact of faster content delivery, and steady demand for AEM developers in India and globally.
behavioralIntermediateDescribe a disagreement with an architect or product owner and how you resolved it.Focus on data, trade-offs, and alignment to customer impact — not winning the argument.

About this data

⏱ 2 min • About

What's researched here and what you should double-check yourself.

📌 Summary
What's researched here and what you should double-check yourself.

Research notes shared with other AEM developers. Use as a starting point — always confirm openings on employer careers pages and job boards.

Career guidance in this playbook reflects practical strategies and research, not guaranteed hiring outcomes. Hiring processes vary by employer, recruiter, role, location, and candidate. Use the guidance as a starting point and adapt it to your situation.

Built while job searching and shared to help other AEM folks. Not affiliated with Adobe or any employer listed here, and not a guarantee of openings. Data can be incomplete or wrong — always confirm on the employer careers page.

This is not a company ranking — every place has pros and cons. "Frequent" means they often post AEM roles, not that they're hiring right now. Links are snapshots and may move. Keep learning and use whatever approach works for you.
